mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series benchmark, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over 10 years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization tightening and pricing power for manufacturers, generating short-term positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative or flat when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% monthly during periods where GDPC1 growth has decelerated for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls provide a reliable leading indicator of economic resilience, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes remains below 0.01, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation print crossing above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% annualized threshold, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures reveals underlying demand strength not captured by headline figures, driving positive equity returns as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample tests over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation rises above trend, markets price in delayed policy normalization and real yield compression, causing short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0%, contradicting the delayed normalization penalty hypothesis.
